name: Backward Lock Step (Back Lock)
preceding_figure_names: Progressive Chasse To Right, Tipsy To L
following_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Tipple Chasse To Right, Running Finish, Tipsy To R
notes:
When one is doing Back Lock, the partner is doing Forward Lock.
The Man (but not the Lady) may use foot rise at the end of 1.
The Footwork would be THT, but left heel must not rise abruptly.
The Lady must release the toe of the left foot from the floor as it starts to move back to become step 3.
Body should be backing between wall and DW throughout.
Lock step has zero turn, more rotation. Rotate more to R.

steps.action: Back in CBMP, backing diagonal wall
Back in BW, backing diagonal wall
Crosses in front of RF, backing diagonal wall
Diag and back, backing diagonal wall
Back in CBMP, backing diagonal wall

name: Change of Direction
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Progressive Chasse To Right, Cross Chasse
notes:
If you don't pull left hip back, it's easy for the step to become back weighted.
After stepping, pull left hip back, leave L knee fwd, to allow using the standing leg to draw R leg.

name: Closed Telemark
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quarter Turn to Right, Forward Lock, Fish Tail, Four Quick Run, Six Quick Run
notes: Footwork same as Walz Closed Telemark. Possible timings: SSSS, SQQS, QQSS (after a reverse pivot). Leader: LF forward (FDC, HT, start to turn L), sway, CBM, rise; RF to side (almost BLOD, T, slightly less than 3/8 between 1-2), sway left, up; LF side forward (DW, TH, slightly over 3/8 between 2-3), sway, up, lower; RF forward in CBMP, OP, DW, H, sway. Follower: RF back (BDC, TH, start to turn L), strong big step, CBM; LF closes to RF (Heel Turn, LOD, HT, 3/8 between 1-2), sway right, continue rise; RF side back (BDW, TH, 3/8 between 2-3), sway, up, lower; LF back in CBMP (BDW, T), sway. Head shape: right then left; keep contact with man; leave room for man's next step; lower before driving into next step.

to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | TH | slightly over 3/8 between 2-3. body turns less

back | BDC | TH | start to turn L
closes to RF (Heel Turn) | LOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2
to side and slightly back | BDW | TH | 3/8 between 2-3, body turns less
back in CBMP | BDW | T

name: Natural Pivot Turn
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Cross Chasse, Fish Tail, Forward Lock, Four Quick Run, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Turn, Progressive Chasse, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish
following_figure_names: Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Rumba Cross
notes: Difference between a Pivot Turn and a Spin Turn is that there is no Rise in Pivot Turn. There is Rise (and brush) in Spin Turn. At corner: turn 3/8 to face DW on new LOD. Turn amount is up to 1/2 to face BLOD, followed by any Natural figure.

steps.action: Forward with RF, FDW, start to turn R, sway, CBM, start rise

name: Natural Spin Turn
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Cross Chasse, Fish Tail, Forward Lock, Four Quick Run, Quarter Turn to Left (Heel Pivot), Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Turn, Progressive Chasse, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish
following_figure_names: Progressive Chasse, Reverse Pivot, Quarter Turn to Left (Heel Pivot), Four Quick Run, V6, Six Quick Run, Hover Corte
notes: Rise on toes, it will help to bring the feet together on 3rd step. Have continuous swing of the leg through the step. When connecting with V6 (QQS lock step), the footwork for this count becomes THT. Keep head to far left, follow L elbow, outside circle. Extend. Collect center back to the spine. Have the middle of body go forward to Man and keep leg moving, but leaving head behind which creates more extension, then head catches up. Don't pause body movement. Simply just leave head behind.

name: Progressive Chasse to Right
preceding_figure_names: Change of Direction, Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Turn With Hesitation, Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Back Lock, Tipple Chasse To Right, Running Finish, Four Quick Run (when preceding figure ends DC or LOD), V6 (6-12, when preceding figure ends DC or LOD), Tipsy To R, Six Quick Run (when preceding figure ends DC)
notes:
Bronze: The Progressive Chassé to R could be started facing LOD and ended backing DW (3/8 turn) when the amounts of turn would be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3 (body turns less), no further turn
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less), slight body turn to L on 3, no further turn.
Silver and Gold: Started facing DC and ended backing LOD (3/8 turn), or started facing DC and ending backing DC (1/2 turn). If 1/2 turn is mad the amounts of turn will be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3, 1/8 between 4-5
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less). Body completes turn on 3, 1/8 between 3-4 (body turns less)

name: Quick Open Reverse
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Forward Lock, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Progressive Chasse, Reverse Pivot, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish
following_figure_names: Progressive Chasse, Reverse Pivot, Four Quick Run, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Hover Corte
notes:
When this is the danced after a Forward Lock, one walk on S is added (Man RF fwd, Lady LF back)
Prepping the walk: get to knees, hip rotate to R, which gives more room to move as well.
When the Progressive Chassé is danced after an underturned Natural Spin Turn or an Impetus Turn as an entry to the Quick Open Reverse, the normal alignment of the Progressive Chassé will be (from Man's view):
1. Backing DC against LOD
2. Pointing DC
3,4,5 Facing DC.
When the Progressive Chassé is taken after a Natural Spin turn danced with normal alignment it will be:
1 Backing DC
2 Pointing to LOD
3,4,5 Facing LOD

name: Rumba Cross
preceding_figure_names: Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Turn
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn (5-11 of Running Right Turn, RF fwd, S, into Tipsy to L), Rumba Cross
notes:
If preceding the Rumba Cross with 1-5 Natural Turn, a sway to L would be used on step 5 which could be held at the beginning of step 1 of the Rumba Cross. This sway assists in maintaining forward movement through the turn. The Rumba Cross may be danced twice.
When preceding with a Natural Pivot Turn, S is added before this Q.
Lady's description includes mental forward feeling, hip to Man, etc.
Do not level up shoulders and hips, leaving R side positive. R knee is forward over the toe, completely balanced on RF.

name: Foxtrot Fallaway with Weave Ending
preceding_figure_names: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Feather Step, Hover Cross, Hover Feather, Natural Hover Telemark, Natural Telemark, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Zig-Zag from PP, Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending, Top Spin
following_figure_names: Three Step, Change Of Direction, Basic Weave (1-4 Reverse Turn to end BDW, check, and Basic Weave), Reverse Turn (1-4 Reverse Turn to end BLOD near corner, check and Basic Weave turning 1/8, 1/8, 3/8; or 1/4, 1/8, 3/4; to end DW of new LOD), Hover Telemark, Reverse Wave, Top Spin (check back after step 6), Outside Swivel (check back after step 6), Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot (at corner)
notes: Head options: can keep head to R all the way till feather finish. Keep volume/space with Man (think about even more shape). Use glute hip more forward to Man, shoulder toward Man (helps to create the PP shape), do not open up side. Stay very left, don't get in front of Man. It feels like Man stays in the fallaway longer (compared to Fallaway and Slip Pivot). Default is a reverse weave action, if leader wants to do fallaway, he will block at the hips, which makes lady open immediately and do a Fallaway. Cushion the knees.

Not listed in "gray book", but often danced.

name: Curved Feather to Back Feather
preceding_figure_names: Open Impetus Turn, Outside Swivel, Reverse Wave, Three Step
following_figure_names: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Open Impetus Turn
notes: Typically, continue with Feather Finish SQQ. If Man shapes, Lady turn her head to R on QQ, keep ribs to Man. Lady can lower her R heel on step 2. Head to L. Hip and body rotating to R, follow Man's hips, rotate hip to Man, connected with Man, hip is still in front of Man, not to the side of Man's hip. Leave RF loosely in front, do not stick R leg too early. Prepare to really use LF to push for next RF fwd step. This is the circling movement action, get weight into heel and then push forward. When swing on Q: do not dip shoulder low, keep shoulders flat, swing forward, traveling. Give center to Man, shape. Breathe, release so body does not stiff up.

(S: 2 beats, Q: 1 beat, &: 1/2 beat) When this figure is danced into corner, start DW or LOD to follow with a Reverse figure, DC of new LOD. name: Hover Feather
preceding_figure_names: Hover Telemark, Natural Turn, Reverse Wave
following_figure_names: Three Step, Reverse Turn, Change Of Direction, Closed Telemark, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Hover Telemark, Reverse Wave, Top Spin, Outside Swivel, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending
notes:
Note -- Body will overturn slightly to R on preceding Heel Pull. the footwork of the Heel Pull will be: 1. TH; 2 H; IE of foot, whole foot, then T of RF and pressure on IE of T of LF.
Note -- Body will overturn to R on preceding Brush step. The Lady will rise from the ball of LF at the end of the Brush step. The L heel does not touch the floor.
The Hover Feather is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.

steps.action: LF diagonal forward preparing to step opposite partner, L side leading
comments: Rise e/o preceding Heel Pull. Up Compress right side, change angle through diagonal, allow head to prepare to go outside partner.

steps.action: RF diagonal back, R side leading
comments: Rise e/o Brush step. Up Feel the left hip is dancing across inside edge of left foot. Keep head very left, feel like dancing rib cage across the feet. Hold diagonal extra shaping for the 2 steps. Push off standing leg to move. Do not take upper body back too fast. Take everything through lower center. Lower center move faster than upper center. Keep counter balance in the Man's arms, leave arms to Man, as you swing rightwards. Pull center back.

steps.action: LF forward
RF back

name: Quick Open Reverse
notes:
Curve the step. Connect R shoulder down to R hip (turning the dial). Preparing for L turn (Lady is inside of turn): Lower part of body is rotary, upper body is on sway plane. For left turning figures, upper body turns less. L shoulder positive.
Keep left side strongly to the Man all through the steps.
The movement is still same direction back, foot is pointing LOD.
Swing L hip and L side through an upward diagonal (push from the R hip), do not put head weight forward too quickly. Feel the lightness. Body stretched.
Changing from L side positive to R side positive.
Feather ending.
Lengthen the R side. Take head to L. Turn the feet. Giving/keeping upper body and arms to partner.
Body position is strongly to the right, head balance on the left.

steps.action: Forward in line of dance, beginning turn to left
Step to side, backing diagonal wall
Step back in CBMP
Step forward in CMBP, finish
RF back in CBMP (small step)
comments: Pull in ab muscle, focus on turning base. Hip creating the rotation.

steps.action: LF fwd in CBMP (small step)
comments: Compact small step. Body turns less. Left side positive. Body feels turned to right.

name: Reverse Wave

notes: Extended Reverse Wave can be performed by adding SQQ after step 6: Man continues BLOD with TH LF back, Q RF back R shoulder leading, Q TH LF back in CBMP; Lady continues facing LOD, poise not back weight, shoulder dancing in back of the feet; then continue from step 4 again. Head can keep left or shape to R. The feeling is legs are dancing ahead of body.

steps.action: LF fwd, start to turn left, Sway(S), CBM, rise e/o 1

name: Top Spin
preceding_figure_names: Basic Weave, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending, Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Feather Step, Hover Feather, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Weave, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Reverse Turn, Closed Telemark, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending, Reverse Wave, Three Step, Change Of Direction, Hover Telemark, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending
notes: On step 1 the footwork of TH may be used. This will often result in a softer and more flowing movement. When this footwork is used the rise will be "Up NFR" on step 2. On step 6 of the preceding Reverse Turn with Feather Ending, Man's body will be facing DW and the Lady will be backing DW. Silver and Gold: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish may be danced turning 1/2 on step 1-3 to back against LOD and 3/8 on Feather Finish to face DC against LOD. Continue with Top Spin to face DW. Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel may be danced, turning only 1/8 to R for Man, 3/8 for Lady on step 7 to follow with Feather Ending DC against LOD into Top Spin.

steps.action: LF back in CBMP, Backing Against LOD
comments: Start turning left hip to left. This is not a large step. Then use CBM.

steps.action: RF back, Backing DW Against LOD
comments: Connect right shoulder down to right side.

steps.action: LF to side and slightly forward, Pointing DC of new LOD
RF fwd in CBMP, OP, Facing DC
comments: See Note for other alignments. Could finish DW of new LOD.

steps.timing: 1/8 to L between preceding step and 1
steps.action: RF fwd in CBMP OP, Facing Against LOD

Connect head, shoulder hip to left foot.

steps.action: RF to side, Backing new LOD

steps.action: LF back in CBMP, Backing DC

steps.action: RF back, Backing DC

name: Tumble Turn
notes: Lady's head starts right, turns to left over first three steps, then turns right on Pivot.

LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP

name: Closed Promenade
preceding_figure_names: Basic Reverse Turn, Chase, Contra Check, Fallaway Four Step, Fallaway Promenade, Five Step, Four Step, Natural Promenade Turn, Natural Twist Turn, Open Finish, Open Promenade, Open Reverse Turn, Lady In Line, Open Reverse Turn, Lady Outside, Oversway, Progressive Link, Progressive Side Step Reverse Turn, Basic Reverse Turn, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Four Step, Outside Swivel, Four Step Change, Brush Tap, Fallaway Four Step, Oversway, Five Step, Contra Check
following_figure_names: Walk (LF), Progressive Side Step, Progressive Link, Back Corte, LF Rock, Open Reverse Turn, Lady Outside, Open Reverse Turn, Lady In Line, Progressive Side Step Reverse Turn, Reverse Outside Swivel, Basic Reverse Turn, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Four Step, Outside Swivel, Four Step Change, Brush Tap, Fallaway Four Step, Oversway, Five Step, Contra Check
notes:
Man's first step needs to be side, not forward, i.e., he does not turn his left foot out to point the direction he is stepping.
Be in sync with lady, flexing knee to start shifting weight (don't tilt body though), moving across the feet.
Rotate right hip to close the lady up.
Do not fall forward with shoulders and upper body.
The feeling is that Lady is dancing at Man's right edge. Upper body is very much behind Man's hip.
Stay connected with Man, keep R side fwd rotated to Man.
RF Toe in line with LF. "side" step is like a forward step. Move "with" the Man.
Do not slide foot. Left foot roll off and end on ball of front of LF, then lift and place LF close to RF (could have a very small gap), slightly forward.
Softer and smoother step, not the sharp accent.
Back to starting foot position.
Note:
Man may turn square to the lady in step 3, to end backing DC. Footwork for man would then be IE BH and for the lady IE of foot.

steps.action: Side step to the left in closed position, along line of dance, pointing diagonal wall
Forward and across step in closed position and contrary body movement position, along line of dance, pointing diagonal wall
comments: Be in sync with lady, flexing knee to start shifting weight (don't tilt body though), moving across the feet.

steps.action: Close to left foot slightly back, facing diagonal wall, whole foot
comments: Rotate right hip to close the lady up.

steps.action: Side step to the right in closed position, along line of dance, pointing diagonal center
comments: Timing is "hold on 1, step on 2": R knee moving forward, body weight shifting with hips. When foot can't hold anymore, lift and strike and place RF out strong.

steps.action: Forward and across step in closed position and contrary body movement position, along line of dance, pointing diagonal center, contrary body movement
comments: Keep L hip "behind" Man's hip. Do not move head. In sync with Man's leg movement, track behind his R leg.

steps.action: Side step to the right and slightly back, backing diagonal wall, inside edge of heel, 1/4 turn to left between steps 2-3
comments: Lift LF, straighten knee (same time with Man), and then place LF (outside edge, roll onto it). Really stretch the R side of body forward, body is quite twisted.

steps.action: Close to right foot slightly forward, backing diagonal wall, whole foot
comments: Do not slide foot. Left foot roll off and end on ball of front of LF, then lift and place LF close to RF (could have a very small gap), slightly forward. Softer and smoother step, not the sharp accent.\nBack to starting foot position.

preceding_figure_names: LF Rock
following_figure_names: Back Corte
RF Rock.notes: Rocks may be danced down LOD or DC. When a RF Rock follows a LF Rock, the first step of the RF Rock will be down LOD. When a Closed Finish follows a LF Rock the first step of the Closed Finish will be DC.

steps.action: back in CBMP | Back LOD | BH
comments: Maintain the body position, rotate only on the man's back steps.

LF fwd in CBMP | Down LOD | H

comments: Very upward, strong stretch on right side.
Styling option: simply staying left, or, look at each other. More neutral position, so you have more to "give" in the next Corte

RF fwd R side leading | Down LOD | H
comments: R side if very "up" to Man. Don't let ab muscle go. Keep core engaged.

RF fwd small step R side leading | Down LOD | H

name: Natural Promenade Turn
preceding_figure_names: Basic Reverse Turn,Chase,Contra Check,Fallaway Four Step,Fallaway Promenade,Five Step,Four Step,Rock Turn (2-7)
following_figure_names: Closed Promenade,Open Promenade,Natural Promenade Turn,Promenade Link,Back Open Promenade,Fallaway Promenade,Rock Turn (2-7)
notes:
Amount of Turn: 3/4 to right.
If the Promenade Turn is danced along the side of the room 3/4 is still made. The following Promenade would be taken to the center, or a slight body turn to R may be used as the LF is place to side of RF to enable the following Promenade to be taken DC.
When the Promenade Turn is followed by the Rock Turn the 4th step of the Promenade Turn will become the 1st step of the Rock Turn, taken DW of the new LOD in CBMP and the placing of the LF to side of the RF will be omitted.
If danced along the side of the room into a Rock Turn the Rock Turn will be commenced DC and if the normal amount of turn is used, will end facing DC. It is however better to turn 1/2 to R between 1-3 of the Rock Turn and end facing DW.
Track Man's hips, stay behind. Depending on intended finishing travel direction, Lady may need to swivel on LF (S) quite a bit.
When this Natural Promenade Turn is followed by steps 2-7 Rock Turn, skip step 4 (placing RF to side), it is more flat and rotational (compared to Twist Turn).
Natural Promenade Turn follows the Fallaway Promenade

steps.action: to side in PP
forward in PP and CBMP
to side and slightly back
comments: Don't pull lady to your lady, instead Man should switch lane.
Stepping back slightly side to leave room for lady to step in between, fully commit the weight so the foot doesn't slip when moving into the 4th step.

steps.action: forward in CBMP, LF placed to side without weight in PP
steps.alignment: DW of new LOD, ending facing DW
comments: Should be strong forward step, as Man is on outside of turn, inside R knee should track the inside of Lady's R knee.
Do not take elbow behind body.

If the Promenade Turn is danced along the side of the room 3/4 is still made. The following Promenade would be taken to the center, or a slight body turn to R may be used as the LF is place to side of RF to enable the following Promenade to be taken DC.

When the Promenade Turn is followed by the Rock Turn the 4th step of the Promenade Turn will become the 1st step of the Rock Turn, taken DW of the new LOD in CBMP and the placing of the LF to side of the RF will be omitted.

If danced along the side of the room into a Rock Turn the Rock Turn will be commenced DC and if the normal amount of turn is used, will end facing DC. It is however better to turn 1/2 to R between 1-3 of the Rock Turn and end facing DW

steps.action: to side in PP
comments: Hip more "up" with Man. Strong step.

steps.action: forward and across in PP and CBMP
comments: Really follow/track Man's leg in time (if it's off time, it'll gap.).
Lower into RF (previous step) to generate power, Extend L leg first, it has a moment of straight leg, before heel touches floor.
Try not to turn the right side at this step (too early).

steps.action: forward between partner's feet
placed to side without weight in PP, ending facing DC of new LOD
steps.footwork: BH then IE of B of RF
comments: Bigger step to give Man room to step his leg. Stay behind Man.

Depending on how much Man rotates, where he puts his foot, Lady will put down foot just a bit late. Because body is turning R, LF appears to be to side and slightly back, then swivel on LF again to Promenade finishing position.
R foot is drawing a circle on floor naturally because of the turn, but don't do intentional ronde.
Lift left side up to Man during turn.
Track Man's hips, stay behind. Depending on intended finishing travel direction, Lady may need to swivel on LF (S) quite a bit.

name: Natural Twist Turn
preceding_figure_names: Fallaway Promenade, Progressive Link
following_figure_names: Closed Promenade, Open Promenade, Natural Promenade Turn, Promenade Link, Back Open Promenade, Fallaway Promenade, Back Corte, LF Rock
notes:
Amount of Turn: A complete turn to the right
Other variations:
7/8 to R could be made to end facing LOD. The following Promenade would move DC.
3/4 to R could be made at a corner or at the side of the room to end facing DC. The following Promenade would move to center.
When following with Back Corté or LF Rock Man will turn a further 1/4 to face partner
Follower: 2 styles (below) to finish this figure, either is ok, but they all finish in the same alignment. (Tendency is LF is not swiveled enough.)
5-step style finish: (traditional way), Lady pivot on LF on step 6.
Pivoting on LF, it needs to pivot enough to have LF finish in the correct position (tendency is underturn).
LF begins swivel when it starts to receive weight, not delay till full weight on it and then swivel. The weight is in transition. When swivel finishes, full weight is transferred onto LF.
4-step style finish, Lady pivot on RF on step 5.
Pivoting on RF on step 5, then place LF parallel to RF in the Promenade Position.

fwd and across in PP and CBMP
to side
crosses behind LF
twist R allowing feet to uncross
comments: Start on B of RF, H of LF

steps.action: weight on RF in PP, ending on whole of RF
comments: End on whole of RF, IE of B of LF

steps.action: to side in PP
fwd and across in PP and CBMP
fwd between partner's feet
fwd preparing to step outside partner L side leading
comments: Keep left side up and forward, do not lift shoulder.
Head turning to PP: Don't turn head to R too early, don't turn head on its own too late either. (Sharpness depends on the lead, could be smooth turn or sharp head turn.)

steps.action: fwd in CBMP, OP
small step in PP

name: Open Finish
preceding_figure_names: LF Rock, Walk (LF), Progressive Side Step, Progressive Link, Open Reverse Turn, Lady Outside, Open Reverse Turn, Lady In Line, Progressive Side Step Reverse Turn, Reverse Outside Swivel, Basic Reverse Turn, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Four Step, Four Step Change, Brush Tap, Fallaway Four Step, Oversway, Five Step, Back Corte, LF Rock, Outside Swivel, Closed Promenade, Open Promenade, Natural Promenade Turn, Promenade Link, Back Open Promenade, Fallaway Promenade
following_figure_names: Walk (LF), Progressive Side Step, Progressive Link, Open Reverse Turn, Lady Outside, Open Reverse Turn, Lady In Line, Progressive Side Step Reverse Turn, Reverse Outside Swivel, Basic Reverse Turn, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Four Step, Four Step Change, Brush Tap, Fallaway Four Step, Oversway, Five Step, Back Corte, LF Rock, Outside Swivel, Closed Promenade, Open Promenade, Natural Promenade Turn, Promenade Link, Back Open Promenade, Fallaway Promenade
notes:
Closed Finish
Finish with feet together (LF is slightly forward, but two feet are closed to each other). e.g. Closed Promenade 
Open Finish
Leader
Q: RF fwd | start from PP
Q: LF side and slightly fwd | Pointing between Wall and DW | IE of foot
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Between wall and DW | H
Follower
Q: LF fwd | start from PP
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing between Wall and DW | IE of BH
S: LF back in CBMP | between Wall and DW | B
Rotate to the R, take R hip back, L leg back in CBMP, landing OE of front of LF.
Keep hips toward Man.
Allow upper body to rotate to R, without becoming back weighted. Never allow body to go behind the hips. 
Practice to not be back weighted: should be easy to do rocking action, keep weight on ball of foot. 
The step becomes BH when next step is a back step. 
It could also be followed an Outside Swivel, in which case heel does not drop, keep weight on ball of LF, L leg/foot compresses and Lady comes back out with a forward step on RF and swivels. 
If doing RF flick, LF heel may kiss the floor but still keep weight more forward and not let upper body fall back behind hips.
Technique Notes
Other alignments and directions may be used.
Amount of turn: slightly less turn to L will be made on a figure when changing a Closed Finish to Open Finish. Although normal turn will be made when following with a curve or turn to L, e.g. Walk on LF, or Outside Swivel turning L from Open Finish (method 2).
Follows can be moving forward, back or to side. When stepping forward from and Open Finish, step 1 will not be taken in CBMP.

side and slightly fwd
fwd in CBMP, OP
fwd
to side and slightly back
back in CBMP

notes:
The figure has solid, sharp feeling, not soft. It is more of an "upward" action with body projecting upward.
90 body rotation in body seems to give the right look and impact.

steps.action: LF forward in CBMP | DW | H | No Turn | No CBM
comments: Turn his frame when his weight is on his left foot, then put down right foot under shoulder, in promenade position.
Very small step, very strong CBMP feel, very tight, using inside L knee next to outside of Lady's L knee, mini contra check, feeling the LF is slightly turned in to help with the rotation into link.
Create torque to wind up lady before turning her.
Turn the lady to Right, not Left.
There is the effect of a windup before the second step.
Keep shoulder flat, do not pop.

steps.action: RF side to side and slightly back in PP | Facing DW | IE of foot and IE of B of LF | 1/4 to R | No CBM
comments: As the second step is taken, the RF should move slightly back and to the side of the left foot.
This step release the tension of the CBM of the last step.
RF steps under right shoulder, frame turns sharply right.
rotate thigh and hips first, then upper torso catches up, but elbow never goes behind body.
Lady is in front Man, but behind right hip.
Frame is turning to R at the same time to lead lady into a link (as opposed to progressive side step).
Head should always be at the same level. Avoid the tendency to lower on a walk, and then rise back up on the side step on the progressive link, which causes right side to go up relative to left side.
do not to raise right shoulder, expand across chest, maintain big frame.

steps.action: RF back in CBMP | BDW | BH | No Turn | No CBM
comments: Start with winding up to left (rotate L hip to L, R hip fwd).
RF strong CBMP (underneath body, crossing in the back more to give Man room to step, otherwise Lady's R leg blocks Man's L leg).
Think about front of lower R thigh is against the back lower L thigh very tightly (that paper in between won't drop, there is a lot of pressure).
R toe in, outside edge first, small step.

The second the RF lands, start rotating to R (the tendency is rotating to R too late, action feels disconnected. Rotation to R, swiveling on RF starts after more than half weight is committed on RF, not when weight is fully committed on RF).
After putting Ball of RF down, R heel will kiss the floor before transfer weight to LF on next step.
Stay very left behind in Man's R arm throughout.
Hip is quite closed up to Man.
To increase impact, turn left first on this step and then sharp turn to R on next step.
It's not just placement of the foot, but also shift of the center.

steps.action: LF side, slightly back in PP | Facing DC | IE BH of LF and IE of B of RF | 1/4 to R
comments: The position of the step is slightly back in relation to the body (after 1/4 turn), not LOD.
Body is more or less facing wall, Hip FDC.
Depending on the direction Man wants to go, this could be either almost in place or side/slightly back step.
Stay on Man's R, stepping a bigger step, to end behind Man in promenade position.
Swivel on Ball of LF. Hip turn 1/4 to R, but body turns 1/8.
Keep body as still as possible. Things happen in base areas.
Promenade finishing position: Rib and hip should rotate 1/8 relative to each other.
L hip is higher (but not in a way falling forward), it enables upper body to forward easily.
You want to dance off the ball of foot, not heel of the foot (so you don't want to sit back and have R hip higher).
LF (yes, LF) should be on the IE of the foot. LF is 45 degrees (DC) off the line of travel (LOD), parallel to RF.
RF is one floor board off to R, giving it room for Man and Lady to step through in the middle.
Bring R side to Man. Upward feeling.
Extend - from 7th vertebra bend back. Connection with Man moves lower to hip/leg

Do not turn away from Man. Right shoulder needs to stay with Man, try to get shoulder to square with Man (end up with small V is ok). Do not turn shoulder out, otherwise, it cancels the effect of extension.
R hand: do not bend wrist back. wrist forward.
L hand: maintain "up and out" position, do not slide.
Head turning: should not be on its own, shouldn't be too late or too early. Turning the head when weight is changing onto LF.
Head change finishes when the action finishes.
The figure has solid, sharp feeling, not soft. It is more of an "upward" action with body projecting upward.
90 body rotation in body seems to give the right look and impact.
Promenade position: Lady's L hip feels like rolled downward and backward, R hip fwd stretched, toward Man.
Hip is level, Lady may feel R hip is higher.
Hips has an diagonal angle to Man. L hip is "released". Upper body is closed more.
Actions are in hips, nothing should affect top shoulder line, arm relaxed, no increased tension.
When moving out of PP, LF fwd, legs are moving, hips don't change, L hip stays in "bent/release" position, don't flatten out L hip when stepping LF fwd.

The movement of this step is a bit more smooth (not as staccato as Closed Promenade, for example.)

name: Progressive Side Step
preceding_figure_names: Back Corte, Brush Tap, Closed Promenade, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Four Step Change, Open Finish, Open Promenade, Outside Swivel, Promenade Link, Reverse Outside Swivel, Rock Turn, Walk

When doing it "side and slightly fwd", the sensation is the lower of R inner thigh forces/pushes L leg to go to side, add torque to next step.
Tango walk, big step. Don't cut it short.

name: Contra Check
preceding_figure_names: Reverse Fleckerl, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Natural Fleckerl, Natural Turn
notes: Commence facing LOD or other alignments. The Viennese Waltz Reverse Turn is a Gold Level figure. Man commences with a forward step in CBMP, weight split, extending right side forward without hip rotation. Transfer weight back to RF backing against LOD, then step back with LF pivoting on toe, ending facing against LOD. Lady mirrors with a back step in CBMP, then transfers weight forward to LF, and finally steps forward between partner's feet ending backing against LOD. Do not peel left side off, keep head space relative to partner, and maintain contact without lifting toe. LF may stay flat or release heel slightly. Stay left to partner, head follows left elbow, look at RF over shoulder.

steps.action: Small forward step in CBMP, foot flat
comments: Lady: RF back small step in CBMP, foot flat, BDC, 1/8 to L.

steps.action: Transfer weight forward to LF, facing against LOD
comments: Lady: Transfer weight forward to LF, facing against LOD, foot flat.

steps.action: Small forward step between partner's feet, ending backing against LOD
comments: Lady: RF fwd small step between Man's feet, FDC, to end backing against LOD, stay flat.
Do not pop up high, stay flat.

Following Figures: Natural Fleckerl, Natural Turn

name: Natural Fleckerl
preceding_figure_names: Contra Check, Natural Fleckerl, Natural Turn
following_figure_names: Natural Fleckerl, Natural Turn
notes:
The figure can commence anywhere where previous figure has ended.
It can rotate 1 up to 2 revolutions depending on the couple.
For both Man and Lady, there is a strong rotation, so there must be a foot swivel on the ball of the flat foot on each step.
Middle of body forward, side to Man. Shaping is not as big as other dances, focus on rotation.
The figure can complete one or two full turns, depending upon the dancers.

steps.action: Forward step between Lady's feet
Side step with weight change
Continue turning on RF, take full weight, LF held behind
Side step to side and slightly forward
Cross behind LF with half weight
comments: Pull ab muscle, keep body lined up and forward.
Do not stick butt out. No big shape.

steps.action: Untwist on RF then step side small step

name: Reverse Fleckerl
preceding_figure_names: Reverse Fleckerl, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Contra Check, Reverse Turn, Reverse Fleckerl
notes: The amount of turn: can do 1 full turn up to 2 full turns (as shown above). For both Man and Lady, there is a strong rotation, so there must be a foot swivel on the ball of the flat foot on each step. Fleckerls are done on the spot, it doesn't move around, focus on rotation. The power for the rotation does not come from upper body. Speed comes from feet and legs. When the steps are repeated, the Man will dance step 1 as lady's step 4 (draw LF back to RF, L heel closes to inside edge of RF). Note: Commencing facing LOD (any other alignment is possible). This figure can turn 1 full turn or up to 2 turns, up to dancers.

End weight on RF

steps.action: LF back, BLOD, TH, start to turn R, Sway(S), CBM
RF closes to LF (Heel Turn), FDC, HT, 3/8 btwn 1-2, Sway(L), rise e/o 2
LF diag. fwd in PP, L side leading, Pointing DC, body facing LOD, slight body turn to R, Sway(S), up, lower e/o 3
RF fwd, FLOD, HT, start to turn R, Sway(S), CBM
LF to side, BDC, T, 3/8 btwn 1-2, Sway(R), rise e/o 2
RF to side in PP, brushed to LF, Pointing Center, moving DC, TH, 3/8 btwn 2-3, body turns less, Sway(S), up, lower e/o 3

name: Open Impetus Turn
preceding_figure_names: Open Telemark
notes:
Ending in Wrong Position: Lady head quadrant should still be on Man's right side, but hips and feet are on wrong side. Do not go too far to Man's left, which will make next step difficult.
Two ways to do shaping:
Slower, more gradual turning head to head over 1-2-3.
Faster, sharper turning: all happens on 1.

steps.action: Step back with left foot, beginning the Open Impetus, maintaining sway
comments: Do not dissolve the sway from previous Natural Turn. Maintain the sway through the heel turn (longer than you normally would.)

steps.action: Close right foot to left foot in a heel turn, preserving sway and rising
comments: Do not lift foot. Keep foot very flat during heel turn, ball of feet just free enough to swivel.
Rise through the diagonal of the body. Rotate the body pass the base and stretch the right side. Head is forward, sending body diagonally back, changing angle.
Count 2: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | FDC | HT | 3/8 btwn 1-2 | Sway(L) | rise e/o 2

steps.action: Step diagonally forward in closed position, continuing sway
Step forward with right foot, beginning the Open Impetus, maintaining sway
comments: This is "Open Impetus". Keep right side positive. Right side connected to Man.
Count 1: RF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM

steps.action: Step to side, preparing for close, maintaining sway
comments: Count 2: LF to side | BDC | T | 3/8 to R btwn 1-2 | Sway(R) | rise e/o 2

Right front is still positive, do not pull R shoulder away. Allow base to turn first, turn feet underneath the body, head the last to turn. Create soft action. Try not to anticipate promenade and turn too early. Delay the turn. Turn through the feet first.

steps.action: Close left foot to right foot without weight, completing the Wing
comments: Count 3: RF to side in PP, having brushed to LF | Pointing to center (moving DC) | TH | 3/8 btwn 2-3, body turns less | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 3
Feel the turn, delay the head.
Develop strong left side stretch at the end.

name: Open Telemark into Cross Hesitation
preceding_figure_names: Closed Change RF, Closed Wing, Double Reverse Spin, Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot, Hesitation Change, Reverse Pivot, Reverse Turn, Wing
following_figure_names: Chasse From Promenade Position, Weave From Promenade Position, Cross Hesitation, Wing, Left Whisk
notes: Cross Hesitation can be used from other positions and can be overturned. If overturned, RF position will be "fwd and across". There is no foot swivel on step 4 unless cross hesitation is overturned. Normal ending is stepping back with Lady outside.

steps.action: Forward step DC, start turn left, sway S, CBM, rise on 1
comments: Has strong swivel action in the base area.

steps.action: Side step BDW, toe T, sway L, up
comments: Man "roll on" the lady, turning without the Lady.

steps.action: Side and slightly forward in PP, point DW, body faces wall, sway S, up, lower on 3
Back step BDC, toe hold, start turn left, sway S, CBM, rise slightly on 1, NFR
Close to RF heel turn, FLOD, sway R, continue rise on 2
comments: Anchor, pull in center. Let base rotate.

steps.action: Diagonal forward in PP, right side leading, point LOD, slight body turn left, sway S, up, lower on 3
comments: Completing the Telemark with body turning to PP.

name: Open Telemark
preceding_figure_names: Back Whisk, Basic Weave, Contra Check, Fallaway Whisk, Open Impetus, Open Telemark, Outside Change, Turning Lock, Turning Lock to R, Weave From Promenade Position, Whisk
following_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Open Telemark, Double Reverse Spin, Progressive Chasse To R, Reverse Turn, Drag Hesitation, Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot, Whisk
notes:
Wing can be used from other positions.
Less turn can be made on Wing to end facing LOD.
See Closed Telemark for leading Lady to do heel turn.

steps.action: Moves forward DC, starts to turn left, sway, CBM, rise
Steps to side BDW, T, sway left, up
Steps to side and slightly forward in PP, pointing DW, body facing wall, sway, up, lower
Moves forward across in PP and CBMP, pointing to LOD, H, sway S
comments: This is "Wing". See Open Impetus and Wing.

steps.action: Closes to RF without weight, pressure on T of RF flat, body turn on 5, sway S, slight rise on 5 and 6, NFR
comments: No foot swivel, but body turn only to face LOD

steps.action: Closes to RF without weight, FDC, same as step 5, sway S
comments: Foot swivel is used to end facing DC

steps.action: Steps back BDC, start to turn left, sway, CBM, rise slightly
comments: This is "Open Telemark". See "Closed Telemark" for leading Lady to do heel turn.

steps.action: Closes to RF (Heel Turn), FLOD, HT, sway R, continue rise
Diagonal forward and slightly to R in PP, R side leading, pointing LOD, slight body turn to L, sway S, up, lower
Moves forward in PP and CBMP, FDC, HT, sway S, CBM, start to rise
Steps forward preparing to step OP on L side, facing Centre, T, sway L, continue to rise
comments: Right side leading. Head up and leftward.

steps.action: Steps forward in CBMP, OP on L side, facing against LOD, continue to turn to BDC, sway L, up, lower

name: Progressive Chasse to Right
preceding_figure_names: Closed Change RF, Closed Wing, Double Reverse Spin, Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot, Hesitation Change, Reverse Pivot, Reverse Turn, Wing, Open Impetus, Outside Spin (underturned), Outside Change (when ending DC)
following_figure_names: Natural Turn (4-6), Hesitation Change (4-6), Closed Impetus, Back Lock, Fallaway Whisk (standard, or with turn at corner), Back Whisk (standard, or with turn at corner), Open Impetus, Outside Spin (underturned), Outside Change (when ending DC)
notes:
Bronze: The Progressive Chassé to R could be started facing LOD and ended backing DW (3/8 turn) when the amounts of turn would be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3 (body turns less), no further turn
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less), slight body turn to L on 3, no further turn.
Silver and Gold: Started facing DC and ended backing LOD (3/8 turn), or started facing DC and ending backing DC (1/2 turn). If 1/2 turn is made the amounts of turn will be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3, 1/8 between 4-5
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less). Body completes turn on 3, 1/8 between 3-4 (body turns less)

steps.action: fwd
to side
closes to RF
to side and slightly back
back
comments: Inside of turn, don't dance too quickly ahead of Man.
Don't stick foot out early, lower body first. let the body initiate smooth movement.
R shoulder and hip connecting down, axis point, strong stretch through left side of body,
Turn your hips from the crossed position

steps.action: closes to LF
comments: Do not rush, stay on top longer, keep with Man.

steps.action: to side and slightly fwd
comments: Finishing position: hips lifted to Man (especially left side), don't just turn upper body.
